title: Mallotus longipes
intro: Mallotus longipes, also known as the Long-stalked Mallotus, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Mallotus longipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mallotus longipes is often made into a tea or tincture, and is also used in capsule or powder form, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its bioactive compounds.
taxonomy: Mallotus longipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. The Euphorbiaceae family includes over 300 genera and over 2,800 species, with many of these species being economically important as food sources or providing valuable timber.
